name: Technical Terms: Official Correspondence (Government Order Passages)

sourceRefs.label: civilserviceaspirants.in - two dedicated TNPSC G.O.-passage MCQ sets (Namma Ooru Clean Project G.O., 10 questions; Water Conservation Initiative G.O., 2 questions plus the same format)

researchNotes: Closes Part C Unit IV (Technical Terms) at 2 topics, matching the original estimate exactly. Real exam evidence shows 'Official Correspondence' is tested as READING COMPREHENSION of an actual Government Order (G.O.) passage — not as isolated term-definitions like the Administrative/Departmental Terms topic. This is a useful preview of the same passage+MCQ format Part C Unit V (Reading Comprehension) will need.

reading.intro: A Government Order (G.O.) is the standard form of official written correspondence by which the Tamil Nadu Government announces, sanctions, or directs an action. TNPSC tests this topic by giving a short G.O. passage and asking direct comprehension questions about its content (what, when, who, how much, how often). Two real G.O. passages are reproduced below for practice.

qa.question: G.O. No. 58 (dated 12.03.2024) passage: "The Government of Tamil Nadu has decided to implement a new initiative called 'Namma Ooru Clean Project' to promote cleanliness and sanitation in urban and rural areas. The project will be launched in all districts from 1st April 2024. Every municipality and panchayat shall appoint sanitation officers to oversee the project's implementation. Separate funds will be allocated to each district based on population and area. Monthly review meetings will be conducted at the district level, and sanitation awareness camps will be organized in schools and public places. The Department of Municipal Administration and Water Supply will monitor the entire execution of the project." — What is the project's name, its launch date, and which department monitors it?
qa.answer: Name: 'Namma Ooru Clean Project'. Launch date: 1st April 2024 (all districts). Monitoring department: Department of Municipal Administration & Water Supply.

qa.question: From the same G.O.: who is appointed to oversee implementation at the local level, and how are funds allocated?
qa.answer: Sanitation Officers are appointed by every municipality and panchayat. Funds are allocated to each district based on BOTH population AND area (not population alone, and not equally).

qa.question: From the same G.O.: how often are review meetings held, where are awareness camps organized, and is the project urban-only?
qa.answer: Review meetings: MONTHLY, at the district level. Awareness camps: in schools and public places. The project covers BOTH urban and rural areas, not urban areas only.

qa.question: G.O. No. 245 (dated 15.05.2024) passage: "The Government of Tamil Nadu has sanctioned the launch of the 'Water Conservation and Management Initiative' aimed at addressing the growing water scarcity issues in both urban and rural areas of the state. This initiative includes the installation of rainwater harvesting systems in all government buildings, schools, and public institutions within the next 12 months..." — What is this initiative's main objective, and where will rainwater harvesting systems be installed?
qa.answer: Main objective: to address water scarcity. Rainwater harvesting systems will be installed in ALL government buildings, schools, and public institutions (not restricted to rural, private, or urban buildings only).

qa.question: From the same G.O.: who leads the oversight task force, what is the initial budget, and how often must local bodies report progress?
qa.answer: A Water Conservation Task Force is created under the Department of Environment and Forests. Initial budget: ₹50 crores for the current financial year. Local bodies (municipalities and panchayats) must submit progress reports MONTHLY.

questions.prompt: As per G.O. No. 58, what is the name of the project?
When will the Namma Ooru Clean Project be launched across districts?
Which department is responsible for monitoring the Namma Ooru Clean Project?
options: Department of Municipal Administration & Water Supply
questions.explanation: Department of Municipal Administration & Water Supply.

questions.prompt: How will funds be allocated to districts under the Namma Ooru Clean Project?
How often will review meetings be conducted under the Namma Ooru Clean Project?
Is the Namma Ooru Clean Project limited to urban areas only?
options: No, it covers both urban and rural areas
questions.explanation: No, covers both urban and rural areas.

questions.prompt: On which date was G.O. No. 58 (Namma Ooru Clean Project) issued?
What is the main objective of the 'Water Conservation and Management Initiative' (G.O. No. 245)?
Where will rainwater harvesting systems be installed as per G.O. No. 245?
options: In all government buildings, schools, and public institutions
questions.explanation: In all government buildings, schools, and public institutions.

questions.prompt: Under G.O. No. 245, which department leads the Water Conservation Task Force?
options: Department of Municipal Administration & Water Supply

questions.prompt: What is the initial budget sanctioned under G.O. No. 245 for the current financial year?
